sentences.romanized: Zhège zhōumò nǐ yǒu kòng ma?
sentences.grammarNote: 这个 (zhège) = 'this' + measure word. 个 is used with 周末 here. 有空 (yǒu kòng) literally means 'have free time' — the standard way to ask if someone is available.

sentences.base_language: I want to see a movie. Do you want to come?
sentences.target_language: 我想去看电影。你想去吗？
sentences.romanized: Wǒ xiǎng qù kàn diànyǐng. Nǐ xiǎng qù ma?
sentences.literal: I want-to go watch movie. You want-to go?

sentences.base_language: What time does the movie start?
sentences.grammarNote: 几点 (jǐ diǎn) = 'what time?' (literally 'how many o'clock'). 几 is used for numbers expected to be small (under 10). For larger quantities, use 多少 instead.
半 (bàn) = 'half' and is used after 点 for 'half past': 七点半 = 7:30. For quarter past use 一刻 (yī kè): 七点一刻 = 7:15.
不要 (bùyào) = 'don't / must not'. It's the standard way to form a negative imperative. 别 (bié) is a common colloquial alternative: 别迟到！
